content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest is note/source-only in the hot path; hard claims/tasks can be produced by later consolidation. Original router reason: Imported document content provides durable conceptual guidance and operational criteria, best stored as reusable notes rather than strict structured facts/tasks.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/task/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create a task unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DIRECTION; Memory updates must not be append-only and require retrieval/reconciliation; The architecture defines a mandatory write pipeline: decide whether to write, find similar/conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DIRECTION; Read path policy maps query types to retrieval modes and capped budgets; Section 12 specifies when memory retrieval is needed, how to choose mode (claim-first, note-first, mixed, evidence-first/fallback), and explicit v3 budget caps per mode; answer composition must distinguish verified vs provisional, handle conflicts, honor scope, abstain when needed, and avoid presenting notes as hard facts.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 12. Read path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended flow routes all write modes through Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer before specialized updaters; The recommended architecture routes noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed decisions from Memory Router, and all non-noop writes pass through Write-Time Retrieval then Entity Canonicalizer before Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, with reconciliation and background workers around shared storage.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DIRECTION; Claim vs Note write policy in memory architecture; The section defines a decision rule: write Claim for explicit, normalizable, precision-answerable information comparable by predicate policy; write Note for important retrieval-relevant context that is too rich or too early for hard facts; write both when material contains explicit facts/tasks plus rationale or direction.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Truth state and Retrieval state are orthogonal status dimensions; The architecture treats Truth state and Retrieval state as separate dimensions: Truth state tracks knowledge validity per record type (different enums for claims and notes), while Retrieval state tracks hot-path/search handling (`active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`). A record can combine both independently (e.g., `resolved`+`warm`, `superseded`+`warm`).; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Memory architecture uses two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths; In Memory architecture v3, writes are split between a direct structured path for safely normalizable items and a note path for important but not-yet-hard knowledge; maintenance is split into consolidation (promoting mature notes) and repair (fixing memory degradation).; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; Responsibility split across runtime, memory worker, and storage layer; Section 10 assigns clear boundaries: User-facing runtime handles dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; Memory worker/manager owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention; Storage layer provides immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<7> DOC_DIGEST; Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture with distinct roles; The architecture separates memory into Tier A (core prompt context), Tier B (typed long-term records), Tier C (rich semantic notes), Tier D (raw evidence), and Tier E (retrieval indexes), with each tier explicitly defining what should and should not be stored there.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<8> DOC_DIGEST; Write path v3 defines an 8-step transactional memory update pipeline; Section 11 defines a strict write pipeline: persist source, route mode, retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, run typed extractors, reconcile with existing items, apply all updates in one transaction, then schedule maintenance.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<9> DOC_DIGEST; Memory architecture v3 is organized around four axes; The architecture defines memory as a 4-axis system (Representation, Lifecycle, Retrieval policy, Scope and lineage) to avoid degenerate designs like transcript dumps or brittle fact stores.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<10> DOC_DIGEST; AI agent memory layer goals emphasize durable, updateable, and traceable memory; The system goal is a memory layer that persists across sessions, stores multiple knowledge types (facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, plans), supports knowledge updates, preserves provenance, avoids writing every turn permanently, and includes aging/archive/repair behaviors.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<11> DIRECTION; Structured scope v3 requires dual scope representation with mandatory scope_json fields; In v3, scope must be stored both as human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; at minimum `scope_json` includes `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation and reduce retrieval overgeneralization.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Note; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Note; reason=Existing concept entity with exact name match and correct type.
- mention=Memory repair; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Memory repair; reason=Durable architectural concept described as a distinct workflow; no safe existing candidate.
- mention=consolidation; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Consolidation; reason=Explicitly contrasted with repair as a distinct type of maintenance work; no exact existing entity named consolidation.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=project:<0>|agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md|Note maturity|Memory repair|consolidation workflow|repair workflow
        text_queries=note lifecycle maturity fresh stabilizing mature consolidated|criteria to increase note maturity retrieval usage multi-source confirmation no conflict survives time recurring rationale|criteria to decrease or close note supersede stale scope no usage weak evidence|memory repair background workflow duplicate notes claims contradictory active items profile drift stale task states bad entity merges note spam budget leakage|repair vs consolidation distinction memory healthier vs richer
        predicate_hints=has_constraint|has_goal

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:3ba1fe9fde8041a82c660a92539e97ff] EXTERNAL

Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.
